pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 32-46 creates table from Production.Product WHERE ListPrice > 0 AND (ProductID % 10) != 0
Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 48-85 creates staging table from Production.Product with mixed operations (updates at 12% increase, no-change rows, and inserts)
Task description lines 19-25 and export_result.sh lines 14-19 verify this table is created and verified
Task description lines 19-25 explicitly defines 8 required columns for Pricing.MergeAuditLog. Verifier script line 66 checks for ColCount >= 8

pi_items.verified_value: AdventureWorks2022 (Microsoft SQL Server official sample OLTP database)
pi_items.source: setup_mssql.sh line 15 downloads from https://github.com/Microsoft/sql-server-samples/releases/download/adventureworks/AdventureWorks2022.bak. Verified via Microsoft Learn documentation.

pi_items.verified_value: Production.Product (real table from AdventureWorks2022 sample database)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 35-85 uses Production.Product table. WebFetch confirmed columns: Name (nvarchar), ListPrice (money), StandardCost (money), SellStartDate (datetime) exist in this table
AdventureWorks2022 is Microsoft's official public sample database. Confirmed via Microsoft Learn and GitHub releases at https://github.com/Microsoft/sql-server-samples/releases/tag/adventureworks

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the AdventureWorks2022 database, Microsoft's official SQL Server sample OLTP database. The task operates on real schema with the Production.Product table (504 products with ListPrice > 0). Target table Pricing.PriceMaster excludes products where ProductID % 10 = 0. Source table Pricing.SupplierPriceUpdate contains: products with 12% price increases (ProductID % 3 = 0), unchanged prices (ProductID % 3 = 1), and new products (ProductID % 10 = 0). The required audit table Pricing.MergeAuditLog must have 8 columns: AuditID, MergeAction, ProductID, ProductName, OldListPrice, NewListPrice, PriceChangePercent, and AuditTimestamp (verified from task description and verifier constraints).
